title: grindelia stricta var. platyphylla
intro: Grindelia stricta var. platyphylla, also known as gumweed, is a biennial plant species that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and is native to the western United States and Canada. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which can provide relief from various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. Grindelia stricta var. platyphylla has been traditionally used as a natural remedy to promote overall health and well-being, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting the immune system.
